TECHS.label: GenAI / LLM
TECHS.desc: Text generation, summarisation, chatbots, document drafting, email triage, knowledge retrieval, policy Q&A
TECHS.considerations: Requires curated knowledge sources, records controls, human review, privacy-by-design prompts and clear disclosure when used for community-facing content.

TECHS.label: Vision-Language
TECHS.desc: Multimodal image+text understanding, document analysis, visual QA, aerial imagery interpretation
TECHS.considerations: Requires quality image/document inputs, accessibility checks, model validation and review for bias in visual interpretation.

TECHS.label: Computer Vision
TECHS.desc: Object detection, classification, counting, tracking, condition assessment from images/video/CCTV
TECHS.considerations: Requires camera governance, signage, privacy impact assessment, retention rules and careful controls for surveillance use cases.

TECHS.label: IoT & Sensors
TECHS.desc: Connected devices, environmental sensors, smart bins, water/flood levels, air quality, noise, soil moisture
TECHS.considerations: Requires device lifecycle management, network coverage, cyber controls, maintenance funding and clear data ownership.

TECHS.label: Advanced Analytics
TECHS.desc: Predictive models, ML, anomaly detection, demand forecasting, clustering, route optimisation, risk scoring
TECHS.considerations: Requires trusted source data, clear data definitions, model monitoring and transparent assumptions for forecasts or risk scoring.

TECHS.label: Agentic AI & Automation
TECHS.desc: Autonomous AI agents that plan and execute multi-step workflows: permit processing, triage, scheduling, routing, evidence packaging, approval chains
TECHS.considerations: Requires workflow controls, approval gates, audit logs, exception handling, cyber review and explicit human accountability.

TECHS.label: Digital Twins
TECHS.desc: Virtual models of physical assets, simulation, scenario planning, 3D city models, infrastructure lifecycle modelling
TECHS.considerations: Requires asset data maturity, GIS/BIM integration, scenario assumptions, platform cost assessment and ongoing model stewardship.

PRODUCTS.label: SenTRACK
PRODUCTS.desc: Waste & operations tracking (bin count, pickup, contamination, illegal dumping)

PRODUCTS.label: SenIQ
PRODUCTS.desc: Data intelligence layer — chat with your data

DOMAINS.label: Community Wellbeing & Social Services
DOMAINS.desc: Health, aged care, children, families, housing, inclusion and public health services supporting vulnerable and diverse communities.

DOMAINS.label: Environment & Sustainability
DOMAINS.desc: Waste, water, greening, biodiversity, coastal management and climate action to protect and sustain the natural environment.

DOMAINS.label: Infrastructure, Assets & Transport
DOMAINS.desc: Roads, footpaths, buildings, fleet, drainage and transport infrastructure — the physical backbone of council operations.

DOMAINS.label: Planning, Building & Development
DOMAINS.desc: Land use strategy, planning permits, building control, heritage protection and development compliance shaping the built environment.

DOMAINS.label: Public Safety, Compliance & Emergency
DOMAINS.desc: Parking, local laws, community safety, amenity, emergency management and environmental health keeping communities safe.

DOMAINS.label: Economy, Culture & Activation
DOMAINS.desc: Business support, tourism, arts, festivals, libraries, sport and recreation driving economic and cultural vitality.

DOMAINS.label: Customer, Engagement & Governance
DOMAINS.desc: Contact centre, digital services, community engagement, communications and democratic governance connecting council to community.

DOMAINS.label: Corporate & Organisational Services
DOMAINS.desc: Finance, people & culture, technology, information management and project delivery powering the organisation.

EVIDENCE.label: Sector evidence
EVIDENCE.note: MAVlab, MAV Procurement and City of Greater Dandenong explored AI and automation use cases for statutory planning and AI procurement support across Victorian councils.
EVIDENCE.source: MAVlab / Greater Dandenong statutory planning AI project

EVIDENCE.label: Sector evidence
EVIDENCE.note: The MAVlab planning work produced AI planning use cases, procurement guidance and a vendor assessment approach for Victorian councils.

EVIDENCE.label: Example
EVIDENCE.note: Australian councils have used smart bin sensors and fill-level data to support more informed waste collection decisions.
EVIDENCE.source: Cassowary Coast / Telstra smart bin case study

EVIDENCE.label: Example
EVIDENCE.note: Wyndham City reports smart bins with sensors that notify staff when nearing full and reduced collection frequency after deployment.

EVIDENCE.label: Example
EVIDENCE.note: City of Melbourne trialled AI scene analytics using cameras as IoT sensors to understand waste disposal behaviour and detect dumping patterns while applying privacy blurring.
EVIDENCE.source: City of Melbourne / Nokia waste analytics trial

EVIDENCE.label: Governance evidence
EVIDENCE.note: OVIC guidance treats CCTV, analytics software, artificial intelligence and monitoring tools as surveillance practices requiring privacy and human-rights enhancing approaches.

EVIDENCE.label: Governance evidence
EVIDENCE.note: Public reporting on Melbourne CCTV expansion highlights privacy and data-risk concerns when council footage is used for broader enforcement purposes.
EVIDENCE.source: ABC reporting on City of Melbourne CCTV proposal

EVIDENCE.label: Example
EVIDENCE.note: City of Melbourne describes using sensors including soil moisture and environmental data to support open-space and city-planning decisions.
EVIDENCE.source: City of Melbourne Sensors in the City

EVIDENCE.label: Example
EVIDENCE.note: City of Melbourne describes combining data such as temperature, pedestrian and cyclist data to understand city life and improve spaces.
EVIDENCE.source: City of Melbourne Sensors in the City

EVIDENCE.label: Opportunity
EVIDENCE.note: Digital twins are included as a future-oriented simulation pattern; local validation and asset/GIS data maturity are required before investment decisions.

EVIDENCE.label: Opportunity
EVIDENCE.note: Strong fit for summarisation, triage and knowledge retrieval; local evidence should be added before presentation as an established deployment.

EVIDENCE.label: Opportunity
EVIDENCE.note: Potential for workflow triage and routing; requires approval gates, audit logs and exception handling before operational use.

TOP_OPPORTUNITIES.rationale: High-volume, document-heavy process with sector-wide Victorian work already exploring responsible AI use cases.
Clear operational signal from smart-bin and sensor deployments: fewer unnecessary collections, better visibility and faster response.
Strong operational fit, with high governance sensitivity due to surveillance, infringement and privacy considerations.
High asset-management value when condition data, imagery and lifecycle planning are connected.
Strong productivity opportunity but requires workflow controls, human approval gates and integration maturity.
Knowledge discovery and classification value, subject to records, privacy and information-governance controls.
High strategic value for climate adaptation, dependent on asset, terrain, drainage and sensor data quality.
Practical use for crowd, amenity, safety and logistics planning where data collection is proportionate and disclosed.

ASSUMPTIONS: This is a generic inner-metro Victorian council model and requires local validation before adoption.
Technology tags show possible opportunity fit, not evidence of proven deployment for every specific service.
Maturity varies materially: analytics, IoT and some computer vision use cases are more deployable than agentic AI or digital twins.
The Sen* product suite is presented only as a vendor solution example and should be treated as a case-study lens, partnership lens or market-scan lens as appropriate.
Data quality, budget, procurement constraints, cyber risk and workforce capability may limit adoption in practice.
GOVERNANCE_DISCLAIMER: All opportunities require governance review, privacy impact assessment where personal information or surveillance is involved, cyber/security review, records assessment, accessibility review, procurement compliance and clear accountability before implementation.
